# Markov-chain convergence diagnostics {#appendix-convergence}

Table \ref{tab:convergence} reports the Gelman--Rubin $\hat{R}$ and
the bulk/tail effective sample sizes for the top-level parameters of
the full state-space specification, evaluated on four independent
Hamiltonian Monte Carlo chains of $2{,}000$ post-warmup iterations
each. All $\hat{R}$ values are below $1.01$ and all effective sample
sizes exceed $400$, indicating that posterior summaries are not
contaminated by between-chain divergence or by autocorrelation between
adjacent draws.

```{r convergence-table, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
conv_raw <- read.csv("data/outputs/t4b/t4b_full_summary.csv",
 stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
 dplyr::filter(!is.na(rhat)) %>%
 dplyr::mutate(group = gsub("\\[.*\\]", "", variable))

# Drop upper-triangular duplicates of the symmetric correlation matrix Omega:
# keep only Omega[2,1], Omega[3,1], Omega[3,2] so each off-diagonal element is
# counted once.
omega_lower_tri <- c("Omega[2,1]", "Omega[3,1]", "Omega[3,2]")
conv_raw <- conv_raw %>%
 dplyr::filter(group != "Omega" | variable %in% omega_lower_tri)

top_groups <- c("r_nat", "K_nat", "B0_nat",
 "sigma_proc", "sigma_obs",
 "rho_sst", "rho_chl", "Omega")

label_map <- c(
 r_nat = "$r_i^{0}$",
 K_nat = "$K_i$",
 B0_nat = "$B_{i,0}$",
 sigma_proc = "$\\sigma_{\\text{proc},i}$",
 sigma_obs = "$\\sigma_{\\text{obs},i}$",
 rho_sst = "$\\rho_i^{SST}$",
 rho_chl = "$\\rho_i^{CHL}$",
 Omega = "$\\Omega$ (off-diagonal)"
)

conv_agg <- conv_raw %>%
 dplyr::filter(group %in% top_groups) %>%
 dplyr::group_by(group) %>%
 dplyr::summarise(
 n = dplyr::n(),
 max_rhat = max(rhat),
 min_ess_bulk = min(ess_bulk),
 min_ess_tail = min(ess_tail),
 .groups = "drop"
 ) %>%
 dplyr::arrange(match(group, top_groups)) %>%
 dplyr::transmute(
 Parameter = label_map[group],
 `$N$` = as.character(n),
 `Max $\\widehat{R}$` = sprintf("%.3f", max_rhat),
 `Min ESS$_{\\text{bulk}}$` = formatC(round(min_ess_bulk), big.mark = ",", format = "d"),
 `Min ESS$_{\\text{tail}}$` = formatC(round(min_ess_tail), big.mark = ",", format = "d")
 )

overall_row <- data.frame(
 Parameter = "\\textit{All top-level parameters}",
 `$N$` = as.character(sum(conv_raw$group %in% top_groups)),
 `Max $\\widehat{R}$` = sprintf("%.3f",
 max(conv_raw$rhat[conv_raw$group %in% top_groups])),
 `Min ESS$_{\\text{bulk}}$` = formatC(round(min(conv_raw$ess_bulk[conv_raw$group %in% top_groups])),
 big.mark = ",", format = "d"),
 `Min ESS$_{\\text{tail}}$` = formatC(round(min(conv_raw$ess_tail[conv_raw$group %in% top_groups])),
 big.mark = ",", format = "d"),
 check.names = FALSE,
 stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

conv_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(conv_agg, overall_row)

kable(conv_tbl, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, escape = FALSE,
 align = "lrrrr",
 caption = "Markov-chain convergence diagnostics for the top-level parameters of the T4b-full state-space specification. \\label{tab:convergence}") %>%
 kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position"), font_size = 10) %>%
 footnote(general = paste(
 "Diagnostics are computed from four chains of 2,000 post-warmup iterations.",
 "Within each parameter family, the table reports the worst case across the three stocks",
 "(or the three off-diagonal entries of Omega): the largest split-Rhat and the smallest",
 "bulk- and tail-effective sample sizes. The diagonal of Omega is fixed at one and is",
 "therefore excluded. The conventional thresholds are Rhat <= 1.01 and ESS >= 400 for",
 "all top-level parameters."),
 general_title = "Note:", escape = TRUE, threeparttable = TRUE)
```
